name: Push
notes: Push — bench/incline press, overhead press, lateral raises, triceps. 8–12 hard sets, 1–2 reps in reserve.

name: Easy run
notes: Easy aerobic — builds your engine and speeds recovery. Keep it conversational in the 5:45–6:15/km range; if unsure, run slower. Shake out from the weekend.

name: VO2 6×800m
notes: VO2 intervals — hard but smooth, around 3–5k effort. Sharpens top-end fitness and running economy so race pace feels easier. Warm up 15 min easy with a few strides; cool down 10 min easy. Main set: 6×800 m at interval pace 4:20–4:30/km, with 2:00 easy jog between reps.

name: Legs
notes: Legs — squat or leg press, RDL/hamstring curl, split squats, calves, core. 8–12 hard sets, 1–2 reps in reserve.

name: Easy bike (cross-train)
notes: Easy 45–60 min spin, low impact after Legs.

name: Pull
notes: Pull — rows, lat pulldown/chins, rear delts, biceps, face pulls. 8–12 hard sets, 1–2 reps in reserve.

name: Tempo 20 min
notes: Threshold — comfortably-hard, roughly 1-hour race effort. Lifts the pace you can hold before lactate builds: the decisive gear for a 30k. Warm up 15 min easy with a few strides; cool down 10 min easy. Main set: 20 min continuous at threshold 4:45–4:55/km. Hold one steady, even effort.

name: Rest
notes: Full rest or 10–15 min mobility. Keep the legs fresh for the long run.

name: Long run 18k
notes: Long run — the backbone of 30k prep, building fatigue resistance. Ease in around 5:45–6:15/km and fuel every 30–40 min. Run the last 3 km at a comfortably-strong steady effort.

name: Recovery run
notes: Recovery jog — deliberately slow (6:15–6:45/km) to flush the legs. Effort over pace. Optional easy 20 min bike instead if the legs are sore.

name: Primer + strides
notes: Easy 5 km + 4×20s strides at 4:20–4:30/km to wake the legs up. Nothing hard — you are two days from the 10k.

name: Legs (deload)
notes: Legs — squat or leg press, RDL/hamstring curl, split squats, calves, core. Deload: light loads, 4–5 easy sets, no soreness. Protect the legs for Saturday.

name: Easy bike (cross-train)
notes: Easy 40 min spin, keep it gentle.

name: Pull
notes: Pull — rows, lat pulldown/chins, rear delts, biceps, face pulls. Maintenance volume (~6–8 sets), keep 2–3 reps in reserve — do not chase PRs during the build.

name: Shakeout + strides
notes: Easy 5 km + 3×20s strides. Loosen up, stay fresh.

name: Rest
notes: Full rest. Hydrate well, easy day on the feet — 10k time trial tomorrow.

name: 🎯 10k GOAL RUN (sub-40 attempt)
notes: Goal 10k time trial — go for it! Off your 1:42 half, mid-40s is today's realistic fitness, so treat 40:00 as the stretch ceiling and chase a big PR. Warm up 15–20 min easy with 3–4 strides. Pacing: even splits — through 5 km around 20:00 (4:00/km) if you're genuinely gunning for sub-40, or 21:00–21:30 for a strong, controlled PR. Hold form when it bites, then empty the tank the last 2 km. Flat course or track. Cool down 10 min easy.

name: Recovery run
notes: Recovery jog — deliberately slow (6:15–6:45/km) to flush the legs. Effort over pace. Easy shakeout the day after your 10k — walk/jog is fine.

name: Push
notes: Push — bench/incline press, overhead press, lateral raises, triceps. Maintenance only: cut sets ~30%, moderate loads, protect the legs for the long run.

name: Easy run
notes: Easy aerobic — builds your engine and speeds recovery. Keep it conversational in the 5:45–6:15/km range; if unsure, run slower. Easy — a big week is coming.

name: Long run 30k (dress rehearsal)
notes: Peak session and full dress rehearsal. Easy for the first 15 km, then 12–15 km at 30k race pace 5:00–5:10/km. Use race kit, race shoes, your gels and your real start-time breakfast — nothing new on race day.

name: Recovery run
notes: Recovery jog — deliberately slow (6:15–6:45/km) to flush the legs. Effort over pace. Optional easy 20 min bike instead if the legs are sore.

name: Push
notes: Push — bench/incline press, overhead press, lateral raises, triceps. Light: upper focus, minimal legs, mobility. Deload week — arrive at the start line fresh.

name: Easy run
notes: Easy aerobic — builds your engine and speeds recovery. Keep it conversational in the 5:45–6:15/km range; if unsure, run slower. Volume drops now — the legs will start feeling springy.

name: Legs

name: Easy bike (cross-train)

name: Pull

name: Long run 20k
notes: Long run — the backbone of 30k prep, building fatigue resistance. Ease in around 5:45–6:15/km and fuel every 30–40 min. Last 6 km at 30k race pace 5:00–5:10/km. Your final longer effort before the race.

name: Recovery run
notes: Recovery jog — deliberately slow (6:15–6:45/km) to flush the legs. Effort over pace. Optional easy 20 min bike instead if the legs are sore.

name: Push (light)
notes: Push — bench/incline press, overhead press, lateral raises, triceps. Deload: 3–4 easy sets, technique only, no soreness. Last lift before the race.

name: Easy + strides
notes: Easy aerobic — builds your engine and speeds recovery. Keep it conversational in the 5:45–6:15/km range; if unsure, run slower. Easy 6 km + 4×20s strides. Stay loose and relaxed.

name: Sharpener 5×2min
notes: Race-week primer to keep the legs snappy — not a workout to fatigue you. Warm up 15 min easy with a few strides; cool down 10 min easy. Main set: 5×2 min at threshold 4:45–4:55/km, 2 min jog between. Stop feeling fresh.

name: Easy bike or rest
notes: Optional easy 30 min spin, or full rest. Listen to the legs.

name: Primer 5k + 2k @ pace
notes: Easy 5 km, then 2 km at 30k race pace 5:00–5:10/km to rehearse the feel, plus 4×20s strides. Legs primed, body rested.

name: Rest
notes: Full rest. Hydrate, top up carbs, lay out your kit and pin your number. Early night.

name: 🏁 30k RACE
notes: Race day! Go out controlled at 30k race pace 5:00–5:10/km (about 2:30–2:34 finish) — resist the fast early crowd. Settle through halfway, then from 22 km start pushing, and empty the tank the last 5–8 km. Fuel every 30–40 min and take fluids at every station. Trust the training — you've earned this.

name: Rest
notes: Recovery: easy walk or a gentle 20 min spin. Refuel, rest, celebrate. 🎉
